    <item>
      <title>NBA playoffs winners, losers: Pistons, Thunder seize control with Game 2 victories</title>
      <description>The second round of the 2026 NBA playoffs continued on Thursday night with the Detroit Pistons outlasting the Cleveland Cavaliers, 107-97, and the Oklahoma City Thunder pulling away late to down the Los Angeles Lakers, 125-107.</description>
      <pubDate>08 May 2026 01:27:34 -0400</pubDate>
      <link>https://www.yardbarker.com/nba/articles/nba_playoffs_winners_losers_pistons_thunder_seize_control_with_game_2_victories/s1_13132_43818591</link>

      <media:content url="http://www.yardbarker.com/media/9/1/91eae0cc0b04e7171b9c824145b618df03dc8cde/thumb_16x9/nba-playoffs-winners-losers-pistons-thunder-seize.jpg">
        <media:credit> Alonzo Adams-Imagn Images </media:credit>
        <media:title> Oklahoma City Thunder guard Ajay Mitchell drives to the basket against the Los Angeles Lakers in the second half during Game 2 of the second round of the 2026 NBA Playoffs at Paycom Center. </media:title>
      </media:content>
      <category>NBA</category>
      <category>Detroit Pistons</category>
      <category>Cleveland Cavaliers</category>
      <category>Oklahoma City Thunder</category>
      <category>Los Angeles Lakers</category>
      <category>Cade Cunningham</category>

    </item>

    <item>
      <title>Knicks' playoff win streak accomplishes rarity for franchise</title>
      <description>The New York Knicks' 108-102 win over the Philadelphia 76ers accomplished far more than just taking a 2-0 series lead in their second-round matchup.</description>
      <pubDate>07 May 2026 13:59:22 -0400</pubDate>
      <link>https://www.yardbarker.com/nba/articles/knicks_playoff_win_streak_accomplishes_rarity_for_franchise/s1_13132_43815045</link>
